name: Pastel De Choclo (Chilean Corn & Meat Pie)
recipeInstructions:
Corn Topping:
Canola oil - 1/2 tbsp
Paprika - 1/8 tsp
Onion, finely chopped - 1 medium
Corn kernels, thawed - 16 oz
Milk, non-fat - ½ cup
Cumin, ground - ¾ tsp
Salt - ¼ tsp
Black pepper - ¼ tsp
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Spray a skillet with non-stick vegetable spray and heat over medium until hot.
2. Cook the chicken breast for 2 minutes on each side. Remove from the skillet and set aside.
3. Add beef to the same skillet; cook, stirring constantly, until no longer pink. Stir in salt, pepper, raisins, and olives. Spoon about a quarter of the mixture into four small, oven-proof bowls or one 8x8-inch baking dish. Place one quarter of the cooked chicken on top of the meat in each bowl.
4. In the same skillet, heat oil and paprika over medium until hot. Add onion and cook, stirring frequently, until soft and translucent, about 5 minutes.
5. While the onions are cooking, combine thawed corn, milk, cumin, salt, and pepper in a food processor; puree.
6. Pour the pureed corn mixture into the skillet with the cooked onion and mix well. Continue cooking for another 5 minutes. Spoon about a quarter of the corn mixture over each bowl of meat.
7. Bake until bubbly and nicely browned, about 35 to 40 minutes.
